title: Hieracium subplanifolium
intro: Hieracium subplanifolium, also known as sub-leafy hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its yellow flowers and flat, lobed leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium subplanifolium include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support digestive health and immune function.
taxonomy: Hieracium subplanifolium is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family, Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which includes over 600 species of plants. As a member of the Hieracium genus, it is also classified within the tribe Cichorieae. The plant's classification is further grouped into the family Asteraceae and the order Asterales. This classification places Hieracium subplanifolium within the broader context of flowering plants, specifically those that produce showy, composite flowers.
properties:
Hieracium subplanifolium, a lesser-known species within the Hieracium genus, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. 
Its leaves and roots have been employed in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including inflammation, fever, and digestive issues.
The plant's therapeutic properties are thought to be attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids.
These compounds have been reported to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities.
Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of Hieracium subplanifolium's therapeutic potential and its potential applications in modern medicine.

side_effects: Hieracium subplanifolium, commonly known as smooth hawk's-beard, is a plant in the dandelion family. As with many plants in this family, Hieracium subplanifolium contains compounds that may cause gastrointestinal upset if ingested in large quantities. These compounds can also cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals. In rare cases, consuming Hieracium subplanifolium may lead to more severe symptoms, including nausea and vomiting. It's essential to exercise caution and consult with a medical professional if you suspect exposure to this plant.
